libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: When generated tests exercise method interception, BytecodeGen, Enhancer, MethodProxy, or other bundled CGLIB paths, configure both JVM test execution and native-image build/run with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`. Prefer public Guice APIs for coverage; if tests directly target bundled internals in 2.9.1, use the relocated `$`-prefixed class names present in the artifact, such as `com.google.inject.internal.util.$MapMaker` and `com.google.inject.internal.cglib.proxy.$MethodProxy`, not the unprefixed 2.1.7 internal names.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing 2.1.7 tests import unprefixed internal classes that are no longer present in the 2.9.1 default jar, which explains the javac failures.
The artifact bundles relocated ASM/CGLIB classes; adding external ASM or CGLIB dependencies would likely be misleading rather than necessary.
SLF4J is optional in the POM; only add it if intentionally testing SLF4J logger injection, not for baseline meaningful coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: sisu-guice 2.9.1 does not need external services or extra Maven dependencies for normal Guice coverage, but AOP/CGLIB coverage on modern JDKs requires opening java.lang because the bundled CGLIB reflects on ClassLoader#defineClass.
